member.name: T:Prime.TestMethods.Array.IO.FileHelper
member.summary: Defines the . This gives a way to cache local files and share them between test instances. Really only useful for the XML Schema, no need to create 1 copy per instance for those. Create an Empty FileHelper() for each file. Then call HasFileChanged() with the remote file path to create a new local version if needed.

member.name: M:Prime.TestMethods.Array.IO.FileHelper.#ctor
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.

member.summary: Gets or sets the current session context.

member.name: M:Prime.TestMethods.Array.IO.FileHelper.HasFileChanged(System.String,System.String@)
member.summary: Checks if the remote file has changed. Uses shared storage as a cache so only one local copy of the file will exist per test program.
member.param: Remote path to the file. Will be run through Prime FileService.GetNormalizedPath().
member.returns: True if the local file has changed since the last time this was called.

member.name: M:Prime.TestMethods.Array.Utilities.RangeToStartAndLengthTuple(System.String)

member.name: M:Prime.TestMethods.Array.Utilities.IsPinNotGroup(System.String,Prime.SessionService.ISessionContextProviderContainer)
member.summary: Verify that the pin name is a valid pin but not a group.

member.returns: true if the pin exists and is not a group, false otherwise.

member.name: M:Prime.TestMethods.Array.Utilities.ResolvePinScope(System.String,System.String@,Prime.SessionService.ISessionContextProviderContainer)
member.summary: Looks for a pin in package and IP scope and returns the one it finds first.
member.param: IP scope to look for the pin if it cannot be found in PKG scope.
(ref) Pin name to look for, will be modified if needed.
member.returns: True if the pin can be found.

member.name: M:Prime.TestMethods.Array.Utilities.ResolveUserVar(System.String,System.String,System.String@)
member.summary: Try to find the HDMT User Variable and return its fully-qualified name. Searches in this order: 1. Global As-Given. 2. With IP + Global. 2.5 With IP + Module from TestName + Global. 3. With IP + Module from Collection + Global.
member.param: The Module of the current test.
(ref) HDMT UserVar name of the format "collection.uservar" with optional IP + Module scoping.
member.returns: true if it can find the UserVar.

member.name: T:Prime.TestMethods.Array.RepairAlgorithm

member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.

member.name: M:Prime.TestMethods.Array.RepairAlgorithm.PrintTable
member.summary: Printing Table Content for Debug purpose.

member.name: M:Prime.TestMethods.Array.RepairAlgorithm.FindSolution
member.summary: Repair an array given a list of defects, and resources.
member.returns: a solution, represtened by map of resource id to value.

member.name: M:Prime.TestMethods.Array.RepairAlgorithm.CalculatePriority(System.String,Prime.TestMethods.Array.ArrayResourcesDb.ResourceInfo)
member.summary: This will calculate distance in tree levels between the resource and the defects that it solves.
member.returns: priority of that resource if it solves the given defect.

member.name: M:Prime.TestMethods.Array.RepairAlgorithm.RemoveSameDefectsInOtherDataRow(System.Data.DataRow,System.String)
member.summary: This functions will remove a defect from a given row (if it exits), and will update the defects number as well. Example: Before: Priority DefectsNumber ResourceId Value DefectsIds . 1 1 3 3 4 . 1 1 3 5 1 . 1 0 3 6 . 2 1 6 2 4 . 2 1 6 4 1 . After: Priority DefectsNumber ResourceId Value DefectsIds . 1 1 3 3 4 . 1 1 3 5 1 . 2 1 6 2 4 . 2 1 6 4 1 .

member.name: M:Prime.TestMethods.Array.RepairAlgorithm.CreateRepairTableSkeleton(System.Data.DataTable)
member.summary: This will create skeleton with needed columns and types to be used by the algorithm.

member.name: M:Prime.TestMethods.Array.RepairAlgorithm.CreateTable
member.summary: This will create the initial table, that will have for each resource and a possible single defect that it can solve, a row in the table. Example:.

member.name: M:Prime.TestMethods.Array.RepairAlgorithm.CreateTableEntries(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: It will create for a given defect and type, new rows in the solution table, for every possible resource that it can solve it. Example: Priority DefectsNumber ResourceId Value DefectsIds . 1 1 3 5 1 . 0 1 1 4 1 . 2 1 6 4 1 . 1 1 3 3 2 . 0 1 1 2 2 . 2 1 6 2 2 . 1 1 3 6 3 . 0 1 1 2 3 . 2 1 6 2 3 . 0 1 2 3 4 . 1 1 3 3 4 . 2 1 6 2 4 . 1 1 5 5 5 . 0 1 4 7 5 . 2 1 6 7 5 .

member.name: M:Prime.TestMethods.Array.RepairAlgorithm.ExtractInfo(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: It will extract the row or column decimal value from a given defect.
member.exception: Invalid type provided=[type], allowed types=[ROW, COLUMN].
member.returns: the value of the needed type on that defect.

member.name: M:Prime.TestMethods.Array.RepairAlgorithm.UseNextResource
member.summary: This fuction will pick the next resource (The one in the lowest priority, with maximum number of defects). and will use it, by removing all rows that use this same resource, and will remove the defects that are solved from the other rows. Note that after this function call, some rows might end with empty defects column. This is known and handled later by another function call in the algorithm. Example: Before: Priority DefectsNumber ResourceId Value DefectsIds . 0 2 1 2 2,3 . 0 1 1 4 1 . 1 2 3 3 2,4 . 1 1 3 5 1 . 1 1 3 6 3 . 2 3 6 2 2,3,4 . 2 1 6 4 1 . After: Priority DefectsNumber ResourceId Value DefectsIds . 1 1 3 3 4 . 1 1 3 5 1 . 1 0 3 6 . 2 1 6 2 4 . 2 1 6 4 1 .

member.name: M:Prime.TestMethods.Array.RepairAlgorithm.MergeRowsOfSameResourceId
member.summary: This will merge data rows of the same resource id, and will concatenate the defect id to same line. Example: Before: Priority DefectsNumber ResourceId Value DefectsIds . 1 1 3 5 1 . 0 1 1 4 1 . 2 1 6 4 1 . 1 1 3 3 2 . 0 1 1 2 2 . 2 1 6 2 2 . 1 1 3 6 3 . 0 1 1 2 3 . 2 1 6 2 3 . 1 1 3 3 4 . 2 1 6 2 4 . After: Priority DefectsNumber ResourceId Value DefectsIds . 0 2 1 2 2,3 . 0 1 1 4 1 . 1 2 3 3 2,4 . 1 1 3 5 1 . 1 1 3 6 3 . 2 3 6 2 2,3,4 . 2 1 6 4 1 .

member.name: M:Prime.TestMethods.Array.RepairAlgorithm.DeleteDataRowsWithZeroDefect
member.summary: This will remove rows with zero defects.

remarks.para: This extension is called during the Verify() method.

member.name: M:Prime.TestMethods.Array.IRepairExtensions.CreateTestInstanceResults
member.summary: Called prior to any test in Execute() method to generate the test instance results object.
member.remarks:
By using this object, user can have access to the following information.
RemainingResources DataTable object that stores the resulted spares per resource.
UnrepairableDefects List containing the unrepairable defect ID and the unrepairable reason.
Defects List of IRepairableVariables that contains all defect definitions parsed by the current test instance.
ExitPort Outgoing exit port for the current test instance.

member.name: M:Prime.TestMethods.Array.IRepairExtensions.CreateArray
member.summary: Creates an IArray implementation.
member.remarks: This extension is called during the Verify() method and its purpose is to populate the target array VariableMapper.

member.summary: Gets the logical addresses for the incoming defects.
member.param: Indicates weather to reverse or not the income data. Default value is set to TRUE.
member.returns: A list of IRepairVariables containing the logical address information for each defect.

member.summary: Executes the repair algorithm.
member.param: Table to apply the algorithm on.
Table that indicates the spares by resource.
Dictionary to map the defect domain groups per resource.
Dictionary to map the defect ignore groups per resource.
Enum to prioritize MaxDefects, remaining spare quantity, and resource order in repair algorithm.
member.returns: A tuple that contains the used resources with its value, the resultant spare by resources, and a table of unrepairable defects.

members.member:
Gets the binary string that is obtained from processing the original string format.
Original string that contains the variable names as placeholders of where the corresponding value needs to be inserted. Dictionary that contains the Variable/Value pairs to be replaced in the string format. String representing a binary value in which all variables from the original string format have been replaced with their corresponding value. Throws exception when the string format contains a variable that does not exist in the valuePerVariable dictionary.>
